extracted_value: Exclusion Criteria: 1. HIV, HBV, active HCV, or active Tuberculosis infection (self-declared or known from medical records). 2. Suspicion and/or confirmed diagnosis of infectious gastroenteritis/ colitis. 3. Active inflammatory disease (e.g., IBD, SLE, JIA, RA, Kawasaki, other vasculitis). 4. Pregnancy- self-reported or medically confirmed. 5. Active malignancy - Cancer diagnosed within the previous six months, recurrent, regionally advanced or metastatic cancer, cancer for which treatment had been administered within six months, or hematological cancer that is not in complete remission. 6. Congenital or acquired immune deficiency (CID). 7. A proven or suspected infection on presentation with Mycobacterial (e.g., MAC, MABC), parasitic or fungal (e.g., Candida, Histoplasma, Aspergillus) pathogen. 8. Patients that have undergone major surgery, significant trauma or burns in the last 7 days. 9. Considered unsuitable for the study by the study team.

Primary Objective

extracted_value: To demonstrate method comparison between MeMed BV® scores obtained by trained operators in laboratory settings using serum samples and MeMed BV FlexTM scores obtained by untrained operators at CLIA-waived settings using VWB and CWB samples from patients suspected of acute bacterial or viral infection.

Secondary Objective

extracted_value: Simulate the impact of the analytical performance of the MeMed BV FlexTM on the diagnostic accuracy of the data from the Apollo study (NCT04690569).

Exploratory Objective

extracted_value: 1. To assess the usability of the MeMed BV FlexTM by untrained operators 2. To demonstrate method comparison between MeMed BV® scores and MeMed BV FlexTM scores using serum samples at laboratory conditions 3. Provide additional supportive information for the analytical equivalence between MeMed BV® score obtained using serum samples and MeMed BV Flex™ scores obtained using VWB and CWB samples in pediatric participants enrolled at clinical sites outside the US.
